title: Silhouette Score
description:
Implement the Silhouette score for clustering quality evaluation.

For each sample i:
- `a(i)` = mean intra-cluster distance to other points in the same cluster
- `b(i)` = mean nearest-cluster distance (smallest mean distance to any other cluster)
- `s(i) = (b(i) - a(i)) / max(a(i), b(i))`

`Silhouette = mean(s(i))` over all samples.

Range: [-1, 1]. Higher is better (tight, well-separated clusters).
starter_code:
import numpy as np

def silhouette_score(X: np.ndarray, labels: np.ndarray) -> float:
    """Compute mean Silhouette score.

    Args:
        X: Data points, shape (n_samples, n_features)
        labels: Cluster assignments, shape (n_samples,)

    Returns:
        Mean silhouette score in [-1, 1]
    """
    pass
solution_code:
import numpy as np

def silhouette_score(X: np.ndarray, labels: np.ndarray) -> float:
    X = np.asarray(X, dtype=float)
    labels = np.asarray(labels)
    n = len(X)
    unique_labels = np.unique(labels)
    scores = []
    for i in range(n):
        own = labels[i]
        own_mask = (labels == own)
        own_mask_copy = own_mask.copy()
        own_mask_copy[i] = False
        if not np.any(own_mask_copy):
            scores.append(0.0)
            continue
        a = np.mean(np.linalg.norm(X[own_mask_copy] - X[i], axis=1))
        b_vals = []
        for c in unique_labels:
            if c == own:
                continue
            c_mask = (labels == c)
            b_vals.append(np.mean(np.linalg.norm(X[c_mask] - X[i], axis=1)))
        b = min(b_vals) if b_vals else 0.0
        denom = max(a, b)
        s = (b - a) / denom if denom > 0 else 0.0
        scores.append(s)
    return float(np.mean(scores))
explanation: The silhouette score measures how similar a point is to its own cluster versus other clusters. s(i)=1 means perfectly clustered; s(i)=0 means on the boundary; s(i)<0 means likely misclassified. It is useful for evaluating clustering without ground truth labels and for choosing the number of clusters.

test_cases.name: Well-separated clusters high score
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
X = np.array([[0.0,0],[0.1,0],[10.0,0],[10.1,0]])
labels = np.array([0,0,1,1])
assert silhouette_score(X, labels) > 0.8

test_cases.name: Single cluster returns 0
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
X = np.array([[0.0,0],[1.0,1],[2.0,2]])
labels = np.array([0,0,0])
assert silhouette_score(X, labels) == 0.0

test_cases.name: Score in [-1, 1]
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
np.random.seed(0)
X = np.random.randn(20, 2)
labels = np.random.randint(0, 3, 20)
result = silhouette_score(X, labels)
assert -1 <= result <= 1

test_cases.name: Overlapping clusters lower score
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
X = np.array([[0.0,0],[0.5,0],[1.0,0],[0.5,0.1]])
labels = np.array([0,0,1,1])
assert silhouette_score(X, labels) < 0.9

hints: a(i) = mean distance to same-cluster points excluding i itself
b(i) = minimum over other clusters of the mean distance to that cluster
